intro: Acrosorus exaltatus, also known as the "tree fern," is a type of plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Acrosorus exaltatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being.
